title: On Kaworu SEELE and Free Will
display_title: On Kaworu SEELE and Free Will
wikitext:
[]: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 11:54 am So, in another forum we've been discussing 24' recently, and of course the subject of Tabris and free will came up. We know that Tabris is the Angel of Free Will. That's fine and dandy, but what does it mean, exactly? I have always taken it to mean that Tabris has more freedom of choice over his actions than the other angels do; he, like humans, can make his own decisions. That's his gig. But what if it isn't? What if the difference between being an Angel of free will and an Angel with free will is significant? It's confusing in the broadcast version; Kaworu looks all heroic, sacrificing himself for the good of humanity and all. But let's look at how things line up in light of DC material: -- SEELE tells Tabris, during their final briefing, that "[the] resurrected body [of Adam] already exists within Ikari." -- This means Tabris knows, must know , that Adam will not be behind the final seal. -- He appears shocked by what he finds, even though he clearly can't be. What is Anno trying to tell us? Here's how I think it all lines up: SEELE doesn't just want the angels defeated. If they wanted that they could have just killed Tabris themselves and been done with it. But I think they had a more elaborate plan in mind here: I think they were moving the final pieces into place for their endgame. It would be foolish to send Tabris into NERV HQ if they thought he could achieve Complementation. Since he knows where Adam is and makes no attempt to reach him, I'm left with the likely possibility that SEELE has managed to repurpose Tabris such that his ultimate goal is altered; instead of Complementation, he now desires death at the hands of the Third Child. Why? Well, this is where Tabris' nature comes into play. He is the Angel of Free Will. That is to say, he is the Angel governing Free Will. He doesn't actually have it himself -- he is a slave to his nature like any other Angel. But he governs it in others; it is his dominion, as it were. And that was the goal with Shinji: he had to set up a scenario such that Shinji would be forced to exercise his free will, and in so doing break the Third Child completely. The result? A completely compliant pilot of Unit 01, one who will not interfere with Instrumentality in any way, shape, or form. And what of the descent through the Geofront? A performance for Rei, who needs to know that "Adam" is really Lilith. This will make it easier for her to perform her necessary role later on down the line. The main problem with this theory? In EoE the JSSDF soldiers storm NERV HQ with explicit instructions to kill the pilots. Why would SEELE send Kaworu to break Shinji and nudge Rei in the general direction of the truth if they planned on killing the Children during the assault? My guess is they didn't. They actually needed the First and Third Children alive, but they couldn't tell the JSSDF as much because it would force them to tip their hand. Instead they just told the military to storm the facility and trusted that NERV would make protecting the pilots a top priority (which did indeed turn out to be the case). The soldiers were acting on orders from their command authority which made killing the kids a primary mission goal: this makes solid tactical sense, since the premise behind the attack is that NERV has betrayed humanity and is hoping to start Third Impact. But that command authority is Japan, not SEELE. SEELE couldn't contradict those orders without raising suspicion, so they just rolled the dice and went with it. (this also explains why they tried the soft approach first; if they can take control of the Magi they don't have to risk the Children in a gamble due to their cover story. But it didn't work so, well, whatever.) Soooo, I think that takes care of all the loose threads. Kaworu is an angel instead of a sympathetic child, SEELE's actions make sense, the plot hole is patched, the JSSDF's orders are explained, and the whole mess starts looking like it actually holds together pretty well. Comments?

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:04 pm Bagheera wrote: But let's look at how things line up in light of DC material: -- SEELE tells Tabris, during their final briefing, that "[the] resurrected body [of Adam] already exists within Ikari." -- This means Tabris knows, must know , that Adam will not be behind the final seal. -- He appears shocked by what he finds, even though he clearly can't be. What is Anno trying to tell us?

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:11 pm Xard wrote: Contradiction didn't exist in On Air version and is result for injecting crucial lolplot information originally in Death & Rebirth.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:15 pm Bagheera wrote: I don't believe you.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:26 pm For whatever it's worth, Kaworu putting on an "act" for Rei/Lilith (and to a lesser extent, Shinji) has been brought up long before, I believe by "old-timers" Shin-Seiki or TheWayneIac . I personally find myself agreeing with that theory. Your additional comments do hold up pretty well, especially with Seele banking on Nerv to protect the pilots which worked out nicely in the end. Although I think it would only be in Shinji's case of needing him in Eva-01, since Seele has no idea whatsoever that Rei = Lilith...until she becomes GNR that is.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Feb 05, 2011 3:18 pm It's worth bringing up that the Angelic titles are only symbolic: They're descriptive, not proscriptive. Kaworu probably gained the title of Tabris because he's effectively a "Nephilim"; he legitimately has claim to both the races of Angel and Lilin, therefore (as if he wasn't enough of a Christ figure), he probably has both Angelic and mortal instincts to some extent. It doesn't mean he has "more free will" (whatever that means) Than the other Angels.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 05, 2011 6:55 pm The only problem with this theory is that it isn't anything that hasn't been offered to try and fix this mess many times before. If you really have a lot of time on your hands, you can read that massive 24 plotholes thread and I'm pretty sure all these theories get brought up. Xard wrote: I cite lack of any widely accepted or plausible theory for existence of plot hole as cirmumstancial evidence

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 7:22 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: The only problem with this theory is that it isn't anything that hasn't been offered to try and fix this mess many times before.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 05, 2011 7:37 pm Bagheera wrote: I guess my question at this point is this: do alternate theories address both the hole and everything surrounding it?

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 7:41 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Yes, but I'd be loath to try and recall all of them right now. There are just so many variables and possible combinations for every single element. Your post didn't even address (for instance) the famous Angel Resurrection theory.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 05, 2011 8:02 pm Bagheera wrote: as far as I can tell the ART never really enters into the plot in any meaningful sense.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 8:12 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Not sure what you mean here...

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Feb 05, 2011 8:29 pm Bagheera wrote: And what of the descent through the Geofront? A performance for Rei, who needs to know that "Adam" is really Lilith. This will make it easier for her to perform her necessary role later on down the line.

[]: Re: On Kaworu, SEELE, and Free Will             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Feb 05, 2011 8:34 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: While most of the original post appears plausible at first glance, this I disagree with. How can you argue [1] that Kaworu has no free will and only acts on SEELE's instructions, while you also argue [2] that he gives a performance to Rei/Lilith, while SEELE doesn't know about Rei's true nature? [1] and [2] contradict one another.
